description: Repeated action done to develop skill or as part of routine; intentional doing. 'Thole' names the doing that shapes—action repeated until it becomes capacity.
sound_symbolism: The word breathes with repetition. 'Tho' opens with thoughtful beginning, practice starting. 'Le' lifts with ongoing effort, skill developing.
grammatical_notes: 'mia thole.' (I practice.) The noun sense, 'thole' (the practice), is the event-noun rule at work. Related to 'shimalo' (discipline): practice is the repeated doing; discipline is the commitment that sustains it.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Practice as path—the repeated action that transforms, not the destination.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Practice as craft—the doing that builds mastery over time.
